chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Cr. B. A. No.2295 of 2025 Date Order with Signature of Judge For hearing of bail application 1.For order on MA No. 12425/25 (urgent) 2.For order on MA No. 12426/25 (exemption) 3.For hearing of bail application 04.09.2025 Mr. Abdul Ghaffar Shar, Advocate a/w the applicants ---------- 1. Urgency is granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all exceptions. 3. Applicants Fahad, Fayaz and Akhtar all sons of Ahmed Shar are present along with their Counsel to seek protective bail to enable them to appear before the Trial Court for seeking appropriate relief. Learned counsel for applicants submits that applicants are innocent and have been falsely implicated by complainant in Crime No. 1288/2025 under Sections 302 and 34 PPC of police station Shah Latif Town and police has shaken hands with complainant party and it is made difficult for applicants to approach the concerned Court for getting appropriate relief as such they have appeared before this Court for seeking protective bail. Without commenting on the merits / demerits of the case, applicants are admitted to protective bail for a period of eight days from today as Friday and Saturday have been declared as public holidays and thereafter there is Sunday to enable them to seek remedy as provided under the law before the relevant Court subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand only) each and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Nazir of this Court. This order shall cease to have its effect on 11.09.2025 or on approaching the applicants before the relevant Court whichever is earlier.

Without commenting on the merits / demerits of the case, applicants are admitted to protective bail for a period of eight days from today as Friday and Saturday have been declared as public holidays and thereafter there is Sunday to enable them to seek remedy as provided under the law before the relevant Court subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand only) each and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Nazir of this Court. This order shall cease to have its effect on 11.09.2025 or on approaching the applicants before the relevant Court whichever is earlier. The applicants are cautioned that this concession of protective bail will not be extended for any reason whatsoever and if the applicants fail to surrender before the relevant Court during validity of this protective bail, the bail bond shall stand forfeited. The criminal bail application is disposed of in the foregoing terms. J U D G E Asif
